source_textbook.name: Gilbert Strang: Introduction to Linear Algebra (18.06)

extracted_concepts.name: eigenvalue
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: A scalar λ such that Ax = λx for some nonzero x

extracted_concepts.name: eigenvector
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: A nonzero vector x satisfying Ax = λx

extracted_concepts.name: eigenvalue equation
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: Ax = λx — the fundamental equation of eigenvalue theory

extracted_concepts.name: diagonalization
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: A = SΛS⁻¹ where S has eigenvectors and Λ has eigenvalues

extracted_concepts.name: spectral theorem
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: Real symmetric A = QΛQ^T with orthogonal Q

extracted_concepts.name: similarity
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: A = PBP⁻¹ means A and B are similar

extracted_relations.evidence: Eigenvalues are solutions to the eigenvalue equation
Eigenvectors are associated with specific eigenvalues
Spectral theorem guarantees orthogonal diagonalization for symmetric matrices
Similar matrices share the same eigenvalues
